name: Sandusky Waterfront and Dock Row
description: The working edge of the lake — a line of timber wharves, fish sheds, and moored freight schooners whose hulls knock against the dock pilings in the northeast chop. One vessel, the schooner Patience, is moored at the far end of the commercial dock with no running lights showing.
atmosphericDetails: The schooner Patience sits low and dark at the end of the dock, her captain visible as a shape in the wheelhouse window — watching the shore road approach.
The revenue cutter Commodore Perry is moored at the customs wharf three hundred yards east; her crew is aboard and the night watch is visible on deck.
The smell of the waterfront is fish brine, wet rope, and the mineral cold of the lake — a smell that the passengers in the barn have been told means they are almost there.

Jerome Lattimore's usual dock station is a fish shed at the near end of the wharf, where he keeps a small kit of dock tools and, beneath the floor planks, a set of free papers and forty dollars in coin.
